claim.observation: Recursive Sleuth Kit fls of the NTFS volume (offset 0), filtered to WinRAR file names, returned four entries, each carrying the '*' deletion marker: Rar.exe (MFT 124369-128-3), UnRAR.exe (MFT 124371-128-3), WinRAR.exe (MFT 124372-128-3), and WinRAR.chm (MFT 124377-128-3). The leading '-/r' type and '*' indicate deleted, unallocated files.
claim.interpretation: A WinRAR installation -- including the Rar.exe command-line archiver, which intruders routinely use to compress (stage) collected data prior to exfiltration -- was present on this file server and has been deleted (its parent directory C:\Program Files (x86)\WinRAR is also deleted, so these now appear as orphaned files). This corroborates, at the file-presence level, the memory-analysis flag that Rar.exe was used for staging on this host. The presence and deletion of the tool does NOT by itself prove the tool was executed; no Prefetch entry and no matching Amcache record were found to confirm execution.
